title: pseudognaphalium sandwicensium var. kilaueanum
intro: Pseudognaphalium sandwicensium var. kilaueanum, also known as Kilauea buttonweed, is a rare and endemic plant species found in Hawaii. It is a type of flowering plant in the aster family, known for its yellow or white flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are believed to provide relief from pain and promote overall well-being.
